subject: ap-chemistry
theme: The arrangement of electrons in bonds and lone pairs determines the 3D geometry and polarity of molecules.
core_concepts: Lewis structures show all valence electrons as bonding pairs and lone pairs; the best structure minimizes formal charges and satisfies the octet rule.
VSEPR theory: electron domains (bonding + lone pairs) arrange to minimize repulsion; lone pairs repel more strongly than bonding pairs, compressing bond angles.
Every single bond is a $\sigma$ bond; double bonds = $1\sigma + 1\pi$; triple bonds = $1\sigma + 2\pi$.
Resonance structures represent delocalized electrons; the actual structure is a hybrid with bond orders between integers.
Molecular polarity depends on both bond polarity (electronegativity difference) AND molecular geometry (dipole vectors must not cancel for a polar molecule).
Lattice energy of ionic compounds increases with higher ionic charges and smaller ionic radii (Coulomb's law).

key_terms.definition: Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion — electron domains arrange to be as far apart as possible
$\text{FC} = V - L - B/2$; best Lewis structure has formal charges closest to zero
Mixing of atomic orbitals ($s$, $p$, $d$) to form new equivalent hybrid orbitals for bonding
Bond formed by head-on orbital overlap along the internuclear axis; found in all single bonds
Bond formed by side-to-side $p$ orbital overlap; found in double and triple bonds
Delocalized electron distribution described by multiple Lewis structures; actual bond order is fractional
Number of bonding pairs shared between two atoms; higher bond order = shorter and stronger bond
Energy to separate ionic solid into gaseous ions; increases with charge magnitude and decreases with ion size

exam_tip: To determine polarity, draw the molecule's 3D shape and check if dipole vectors cancel — $\text{CO}_2$ has polar bonds but is nonpolar because the linear geometry creates exact cancellation.
